entries.definition:
God and the believers but they only deceive themselves.
خَادِع khādiʿ [act. part.] one who deceives, one who causes
others to be deceived (4:142) إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ يُخَادِعُونَ اللَّهَ وَهُوَ خَادِعُهُمْ the
hypocrites try to deceive God, but it is He who causes them to be
deceived.
entries.definition_english:
God and the believers but they only deceive themselves.
خَادِع khādiʿ [act. part.] one who deceives, one who causes
others to be deceived (4:142) إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ يُخَادِعُونَ اللَّهَ وَهُوَ خَادِعُهُمْ the
hypocrites try to deceive God, but it is He who causes them to be
deceived.

entries.definition:
kh–d–n friend, companion, confidant; to take a friend; lover.
Of this root, أَخْدَان ʾakhdān, occurs twice in the Qur’an.

أَخْدَان ʾakhdān [pl. of quasi-act. part./n. خِدْن khidn] friend,
companion, lover (4:25) وَآتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ مُحْصَنَاتٍ غَيْرَ مُسَافِحَاتٍ
وَلَا مُتَّخِذَاتِ أَخْدَانٍ give them their dowries according to the norms
[governing marriage practices, taking them] as married women,
but not [as] adulteresses or those taking lovers.
entries.definition_english:
